reasoning: The risk subindex has been on a secular downward trend from April (-0.393) to October (-0.576), indicating loosening financial risk conditions. In the final weeks of October, the subindex has flattened, oscillating tightly between -0.577 and -0.576. For the question to resolve to YES, the index must strictly increase from its 2025-10-26 baseline. Because any flat result (i.e. remaining at -0.576) acts as a NO, and short-term momentum favors flat-to-downward movement, the probability of a strict increase in the nearest timeframe is less than 0.5. As the forecast horizon extends to January 2026, mean reversion and the accumulation of macroeconomic noise increase the likelihood of the index breaking out of its current localized bottom, pushing the probability of an increase closer to a coin flip (0.50).
batch_queries: Will the Chicago Fed's National Financial Conditions Risk Subindex have increased by each resolution date listed below as compared to its value on 2025-10-26?
